similar_features.argument_parsing_dominant_pattern: Positional prefix tokens (e.g. `backlog:<marker>`, `specs/epic-`, `@`), plain bare tokens (e.g. `init`, `resolve`, `proposed`), and colon-joined key:value pairs (e.g. `affects:paths`, `supersedes:ADR-NNN`). The only `--` flag in the entire corpus is `--no-adopt` in `plugins/base/commands/rebase-incremental.md:17` — the anomaly.

mode_dispatch_canonical_example.shape: IF arg starts-with X → mode=A; ELSE IF starts-with Y → mode=B; ELSE IF .md or @ → mode=C; ELSE IF empty → mode=D; ELSE → mode=E.

similar_features.askuserquestion_convention: No JSON schema is described in any command or skill. All usages are prose-only — numbered or lettered option lists. The literal word `multiSelect` appears once across the corpus (`rebase-incremental.md:180`) and is still prose direction. Downstream stories MUST NOT assume a typed schema contract; the lead translates prose intent into the `AskUserQuestion` tool's actual JSON.

existing_next_md_top3.current_shape: Implicit ordered sequence from a document-order walk. No explicit array. AskUserQuestion has fixed 3 options (dispatch #1, pick different, abort) regardless of how many candidates exist.

similar_features.anchor_line_window_read: Novel — no prior command uses an explicit `offset = max(1, line-10)`, `limit = 21` formula. Prior bounded reads use `awk` / `grep` (`feature.md:179`), prose ('read only the Status line', `orient/SKILL.md:158`), or budget-based bounds ('≤5 tool calls per AC', `feature.md` RECONCILE). The Step 4a window read is the first explicit Read-offset arithmetic in the corpus.

plugin_base_layout.commands/: User-invocable entry points. .md files with YAML frontmatter; auto-registered (file-presence-based, no manifest enumeration).
plugin_base_layout.agents/: Subagent blueprints. Spawned ONLY via `Agent(subagent_type: base:<name>)`. Role-name `SendMessage` to a base:* name silently no-ops (no TeamCreate teammate exists for those names).
plugin_base_layout.skills/: Two roles: (a) reference docs read by leads/agents (e.g. `base:spec-template`'s SKILL.md cited by `feature.md:170`); (b) utility workflows invoked via `Skill("base:<name>")`.
plugin_base_layout.schemas/: JSON Schema files. `stories.schema.json` enforces `^AC-[A-Z]+-[0-9]+$` for AC IDs; `result.schema.json` carries the per-story result + retrospective shape.

next_md_dispatch_graph.callers: Direct user invocation only. No command in `plugins/base/` calls `/base:next` programmatically.
next_md_dispatch_graph.callees: Skill("base:feature", args: "backlog:<marker>") or Skill("base:bug", args: "backlog:<marker>") per `next.md:141-143`. No Agent spawns, no TeamCreate, no SendMessage.
next_md_dispatch_graph.recursion_risk: None — `next.md:11` declares the dispatcher does one Skill call and exits.

architecture.backlog_triangle_lifecycle: BACKLOG.md ## Findings is the working set (soft cap ~15); ## Archive is append-only rejection history. Writers: `base:project-curator` (proposes mutations), the lead at `feature.md` Step 3/Step 6.1 (epic bullet + pending finding consumption), `base:backlog` skill (user-invoked add/resolve), `bug.md` Step 4 (deferred bullet removal). Readers: `/base:orient`, `/base:next`, `/base:feature backlog:...`, `/base:bug backlog:...`. Four resolution paths: `done→spec`, `done-mechanical`, `rejected`, `promoted`.

packaging_and_release.manifest: .claude-plugin/plugin.json — name/version only, no commands list. Commands auto-discovered by file presence.
packaging_and_release.ci: None. No .github/, no .husky/, no precommit hook. Release is `node scripts/release.mjs` — interactive, manual, creates an annotated tag `<plugin>/v<version>` and a commit `Release <plugin> v<version>`.
packaging_and_release.trigger_on_md_edit: None — edits to `plugins/base/commands/*.md` do not trigger any automation.

spec_format_authority.spec_md_conformance: READY — all required sections present in canonical order; `## Constrained by ADRs` present with `_no ADRs apply_`; `## Amendments` absent (correct — added only on first amendment).
spec_format_authority.ac_md_state: Stub from spec-template — placeholder ACs only. `base:story-planner` Mode 1 will populate this in Step 4 from the spec's `## Stories` previews. Spec's ## Stories references 14 AC IDs (AC-MODE-1..3, AC-STRUCT-1, AC-AUTO-1..3, AC-DETAIL-1..3, AC-PARA-1..3, AC-AMEND-1..2, AC-INV-1) that the planner is expected to materialise.

testing_and_conventions.test_infrastructure: None. No tests/ dir, no .github/workflows, no .husky/, no test/lint scripts in package.json, no Makefile test target, no CONTRIBUTING or smoke-test instruction file.
testing_and_conventions.what_test_means_for_md_changes: The `base:verification-examiner` performs textual presence/absence assertions via Read on the modified Markdown file. Pattern observed in `specs/epic-pipeline-autonomous-retros/S1-curator-refactor/verification.json`: every VQ resolves to 'Does <file> [contain | not contain] phrase X?' with `check_method: read <file> and verify (a) absence of … (b) presence of …'.
testing_and_conventions.examiner_protocol_for_md: The SPEC question template (`verification-examiner.md:30-44`) hard-requires (a) production code at file:line, (b) tests at file:line, (c) confirmation tests are not mock-spy proxies. For Markdown stories, (a) = the prompt file, (b)/(c) = the examiner's own read-based assertion. No formal carve-out in the agent prompt; examiners improvise.
testing_and_conventions.story_planner_for_md_stories: Mode 3 templates verification questions in code-centric terms but is observed (in S1 of pipeline-autonomous-retros) to emit SPEC questions with `check_method` set to a direct Read-and-verify instruction for Markdown files. No `skills/languages/markdown.md` exists — language detection on package.json picks JS/TS, whose conventions are irrelevant here.

command_md_file_structure.title_pattern: # <Title> followed immediately by a role statement sentence
command_md_file_structure.step_heading: `## Step N: <Verb Noun>` at top level; `### Step N.N: ...` for sub-steps when needed
command_md_file_structure.step_body: Fenced code blocks for pseudocode and bash; prose for narrative steps
command_md_file_structure.exit_phrasing: Inline imperative ('exit with:', 'stop and explain'); the terminal step is often labeled `## Step N: Return` and ends with a plain-English summary
command_md_file_structure.retro_block: Subagent convention only — never appears in command .md files

implementation_constraints: Step 0 argument parsing MUST follow the prefix/bare-token/colon-value precedent (NO `--auto` flag). Match `feature.md:52-63` and `backlog/SKILL.md:37-43` shape.

Step 4 detail-branch AskUserQuestion options MUST be described in prose with per-candidate options (`Dispatch #1`, `Dispatch #2` (only when #2 exists), `Dispatch #3` (only when #3 exists), `Abort`) — NOT a fixed 3-option shape like today's gate.
Paragraph synthesis Read window MUST be specified with explicit `offset = max(1, line-10)`, `limit = 21` arithmetic — this is novel; the convention is being introduced by this epic.
Frontmatter `allowed-tools` MUST gain `Grep` for the optional 'find nearest preceding heading' step in Step 4a.

Verification for this epic's stories will be Read-based textual assertion against `plugins/base/commands/next.md` and `specs/epic-base-next/{spec.md, acceptance-criteria.md}`. The `base:verification-examiner` improvises (a)/(b)/(c) accordingly; story-planner Mode 3 must phrase pre-impl questions as 'does the file at <path>:<line-range> contain/not-contain <clause>?'.
Release is out of scope for the epic — version bumps are manual via `scripts/release.mjs` and the user owns that process (per CLAUDE.md: 'NEVER bump versions on your own').

exploration_retros.flag: AskUserQuestion has no JSON schema in any command or skill — all usages are prose-only — so 'quote the exact JSON shape' had no answer; downstream stories must not assume a typed schema contract exists to match.

The epic-next-modes acceptance-criteria.md is the unfilled spec-template scaffold — spec.md references 14 named AC IDs that do not yet exist in the file. This is by design (pre-implementation state, phase: SPEC_VALIDATED), but it is the kind of upstream gap that surfaces during /base:feature Step 2 validation and base:story-planner Mode 2, and the architect needs to know it is intentional rather than a validation failure before proceeding.

verification-examiner.md hard-requires '(b) tests at file:line' and '(c) confirmation tests are not pure mock-spy proxies' — language written for executable code. For Markdown-editing stories there is no test runner, no baseline.json run, and no proxy/behavioral distinction. The pipeline applies the protocol anyway (improvised by examiners), but the gap is structural: no skills/languages/markdown.md, story-planner picks JS/TS conventions from package.json. Worth a downstream finding — either ship a markdown.md language skill or add a Markdown-aware carve-out to the AC question template.
